intro: Athrumpylum yunnanense, also known as Yunnan holly fern, is a type of fern native to the mountainous regions of southern China. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Athrumpylum yunnanense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, this plant is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or extract, and is commonly used to treat coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory complaints.
